name: W5 Push Morning Flow (Shuffle)
description: 12 min. Push press is FIRST today — this flow prioritizes overhead mobility and thoracic rotation. Get those shoulders ready to find out if 50/side goes overhead with fresh muscles.

exercises.notes: 30s each side. Low lunge, twist and reach overhead. Opens hip flexors + thoracic — exactly where push press lockout comes from.
Back flat, arms slide up. Extra reps this week — overhead range is the priority.
NEW — hands on floor, hips high, press straight up. Mimics push press shoulder angle. Just 6 easy ones to wake up the overhead pressing muscles.
Standard, full ROM. Chest activation for later.
Light band. Rear delts and scaps — balances all the pressing you're about to do.
Wall or doorframe. Open the pecs before loading them.
30s each side. Thoracic rotation — third week in a row. Should be getting deeper each time.
Gentle backbend. Opens chest and hip flexors to close.

tips: 12 min — overhead mobility is the priority since push press is first today.
Pike push-ups are new — they activate the overhead pressing pattern. Don't go hard, just feel it.
Wall angels: if you're getting deeper than week 1, your thoracic mobility is improving. That helps push press.
If short on time: overhead prep block only. 4 min minimum.
